---
title: "Attention Is All You Need"
subtitle: "Vaswani et al., 2017 — a slide summary"
format:
  revealjs:
    liveurl:
      button: true
      shortcut: "u"
      position: "right"
      width: "45vw"
      mode: "auto"
      sticky: true
      global:
        label: "Paper (HTML)"
        url: "https://arxiv.org/html/1706.03762v7"
revealjs-plugins:
  - liveurl
---

## About this deck

These slides summarise [*Attention Is All You Need*](https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.03762)
(Vaswani et al., NeurIPS 2017) — the paper that introduced the **Transformer**.

The full paper is always one keypress away:

- Press **`u`** to open it in the side panel
- The panel stays open as you advance through slides (`sticky` mode)
- Scroll to any section and your position is preserved across slides

## The Problem: Sequential Bottlenecks {data-liveurl="https://arxiv.org/html/1706.03762v7#S1"}

Before Transformers, sequence modelling relied on **RNNs and LSTMs**.

**Key limitations:**

- Hidden state $h_t$ depends on $h_{t-1}$ → computation is **inherently sequential**
- Long sequences → vanishing gradients, forgetting early context
- Hard to parallelise → slow to train on modern hardware

> *"The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or
> convolutional neural networks."* — §1

Transformers eliminate recurrence entirely. Every position attends to every
other position **in parallel**.

## The Core Idea: Self-Attention {data-liveurl="https://arxiv.org/html/1706.03762v7#S3.SS2"}

Instead of passing state through time, each token **directly attends** to all
other tokens in the sequence.

**Three learned projections per token:**

| Matrix | Role |
|--------|------|
| **Q** (Query) | What am I looking for? |
| **K** (Key)   | What do I offer? |
| **V** (Value) | What do I contribute if selected? |

**Scaled dot-product attention:**

$$\text{Attention}(Q, K, V) = \text{softmax}\!\left(\frac{QK^\top}{\sqrt{d_k}}\right)V$$

The $\sqrt{d_k}$ scaling prevents the dot products from growing too large and
pushing softmax into regions of tiny gradients.

## Multi-Head Attention {data-liveurl="https://arxiv.org/html/1706.03762v7#S3.SS2.SSS2"}

Running attention once captures one type of relationship. **Multi-head attention**
runs it $h$ times in parallel with different learned projections.

$$\text{MultiHead}(Q,K,V) = \text{Concat}(\text{head}_1, \ldots, \text{head}_h)\,W^O$$

$$\text{head}_i = \text{Attention}(QW^Q_i,\, KW^K_i,\, VW^V_i)$$

**The paper uses $h = 8$ heads** with $d_k = d_v = 64$.

Each head can learn a different aspect of context — syntax, coreference,
long-range dependencies — simultaneously.

## The Transformer Architecture {data-liveurl="https://arxiv.org/html/1706.03762v7#S3"}

::: {.liveurl}
- [Figure 1 — Full Architecture](https://arxiv.org/html/1706.03762v7#S3)
:::

The model follows an **encoder–decoder** design, used here for translation.

| Component | Layers |
|-----------|--------|
| Encoder | 6 identical layers |
| Decoder | 6 identical layers |

Each layer has two sub-layers:

1. **Multi-head self-attention**
2. **Position-wise feed-forward network** (two linear transforms, ReLU in between)

Every sub-layer wraps a **residual connection** followed by **layer normalisation**:
$$\text{LayerNorm}(x + \text{Sublayer}(x))$$

## Encoder in Detail {data-liveurl="https://arxiv.org/html/1706.03762v7#S3.SS1"}

The encoder maps an input sequence $(x_1, \ldots, x_n)$ to continuous
representations $\mathbf{z} = (z_1, \ldots, z_n)$.

**Each of the 6 encoder layers:**

```
Input embeddings + positional encoding
        ↓
[Multi-Head Self-Attention] → Add & Norm
        ↓
[Feed-Forward Network]      → Add & Norm
        ↓
Output to next layer (or decoder)
```

Self-attention here is **bidirectional** — every position can attend to
every other position in both directions.

## Decoder in Detail {data-liveurl="https://arxiv.org/html/1706.03762v7#S3.SS1"}

The decoder generates the output sequence one token at a time.

**Each of the 6 decoder layers has three sub-layers:**

```
Output embeddings + positional encoding
        ↓
[Masked Multi-Head Self-Attention] → Add & Norm   ← prevents attending to future
        ↓
[Cross-Attention over encoder output] → Add & Norm ← connects encoder to decoder
        ↓
[Feed-Forward Network]               → Add & Norm
```

**Masking** ensures position $i$ can only attend to positions $< i$,
preserving the auto-regressive property during training.

## Positional Encoding {data-liveurl="https://arxiv.org/html/1706.03762v7#S3.SS5"}

Attention is **permutation-invariant** — it has no built-in sense of order.
Positional encodings inject sequence position information.

The paper uses fixed sine and cosine functions:

$$PE_{(pos,\, 2i)}   = \sin\!\left(\frac{pos}{10000^{2i/d_{\text{model}}}}\right)$$
$$PE_{(pos,\, 2i+1)} = \cos\!\left(\frac{pos}{10000^{2i/d_{\text{model}}}}\right)$$

Added directly to the token embeddings before the first layer.

**Why sinusoids?** Each frequency allows the model to easily learn to attend
by relative position, and the encoding generalises to sequence lengths unseen
during training.

## Training & Results {data-liveurl="https://arxiv.org/html/1706.03762v7#S5"}

::: {.liveurl}
- [§5 Training](https://arxiv.org/html/1706.03762v7#S5)
- [§6 Results](https://arxiv.org/html/1706.03762v7#S6)
:::

**Training setup:**

- Adam optimiser with warm-up learning rate schedule
- Dropout (rate 0.1) on sub-layer outputs and embeddings
- Label smoothing ($\epsilon_{ls} = 0.1$)
- Trained on 8 × P100 GPUs

**Translation benchmarks (WMT 2014):**

| Task | BLEU | Previous SOTA |
|------|------|---------------|
| English → German | **28.4** | 26.4 |
| English → French | **41.0** | 40.5 |

Trained in **12 hours** vs. days for prior ensemble models.

## Why It Mattered

The Transformer became the backbone of essentially every major language model
that followed.

**Direct descendants:**

| Model | Year | Built on |
|-------|------|----------|
| BERT | 2018 | Encoder only |
| GPT series | 2018–present | Decoder only |
| T5 | 2019 | Encoder–decoder |
| Vision Transformer (ViT) | 2020 | Encoder, image patches |
| LLaMA, Mistral, Claude… | 2023–present | Decoder only |

> *The self-attention mechanism is now the default building block for
> sequence modelling across language, vision, audio, and biology.*

The paper has accumulated over **100,000 citations** as of 2024.
